content: {"key_findings": ["The NIH Research Matters Oct 2024 Panda/Taub TRE-in-MetS study showed modest metabolic benefit in an 8-10h eating window over 3 months, extending TRE evidence from obesity-only to MetS+prediabetes.", "ScienceDirect and BMC Endocrine Disorders 2025 pooled analyses (MD -1.04 kg, 95% CI -2.43 to 0.34, Egger P=0.861) show IF does NOT significantly outperform calorie restriction when calories are matched — critical methodological finding.", "GRADE rating was medium quality in calorie-matched subgroups, indicating moderate confidence.", "Existing PMC and JAMA Network Open sources contribute to the peer-reviewed corroboration base.", "News-Medical.net news coverage confirms mainstream uptake of the Panda/Taub TRE-in-MetS result."], "perspective_gaps": [], "web_queries": [], "academic_queries": [], "exa_queries": [], "convergence_assessment": "saturated", "should_continue": false, "reasoning": "This is a secondary/gap search loop — the parent pipeline already saturated the main agentic search with full 9/9 STORM perspective coverage across 4 rounds and 24 pages in the previous cycle. The 6 additional TRE-specific pages read here converge on the same core finding pattern (short-term benefit in TRE MetS populations, no significant advantage over calorie restriction when calories are matched). Additional rounds would produce diminishing returns. Proceeding to synthesis.", "knowledge_gaps": ["This secondary gap loop re-confirmed existing findings rather than surfacing new knowledge gaps — the main research question is already well-characterized by the parent loop's 24-page read set and 5-persona STORM interviews."]}
